Day by Day
Your Detailed Itinerary
Day 01
May 20
Chongqing Arrival
▾
🗺Arrival in Chongqing
✈ TransportArrival (no specific mode stated)
Chongqing — China’s 8D 'Internet-famous City'
Liziba Railway Station — Monorail through residential building
Jiefangbei — Historic monument amid skyscrapers and snack streets
Hongyadong — 11-story stilt-house complex with Spirited Away-like night scenery
Qiansimen Bridge — Cable-stayed bridge with panoramic views of Hongyadong’s skyline

Welcome to Chongqing, China’s famous 8D 'Internet-famous City' in the southwest. Built on mountains and rivers, it features mind-bending 3D urban landscapes where roads and buildings stack vertically, confusing even GPS. Its surreal architecture, stunning night views, and spicy hotpot have made it a global viral sensation.

Day 02
May 21
Chongqing Sightseeing
▾
🗺Liziba Railway Station → Jiefangbei → Hongyadong → Qiansimen Bridge
✈ TransportPrivate car
Liziba Railway Station — A viral landmark where the monorail runs directly through a residential building, a symbol of Chongqing’s wild 8D design
Jiefangbei — The bustling heart of Chongqing, with a historic monument surrounded by skyscrapers, shopping malls, and local snack streets
Hongyadong — An 11-story stilt-house complex famous for its Spirited Away-like night scenery, one of the city’s most photographed spots
Qiansimen Bridge — A dramatic cable-stayed bridge over the Jialing River, offering the best panoramic views of Hongyadong’s glowing skyline after dark

Explore Chongqing’s most iconic landmarks showcasing its unique 8D urban landscape. Start at the viral Liziba Railway Station, then head to the vibrant Jiefangbei district, followed by the fairy-tale Hongyadong stilt complex, and conclude with sweeping nighttime views from Qiansimen Bridge over the Jialing River.

Day 03
May 22
Chongqing → Zhangjiajie
▾
🗺Chongqing → Zhangjiajie (high-speed train)
✈ TransportHigh-speed train
🍽 MealsDinner
High-speed train journey — 3—4 hours through scenic southwestern China
Zhangjiajie — Dramatic quartz sandstone peaks in Hunan Province
Tujia cuisine — Local ethnic minority dishes sampled in the evening

Take a 3–4 hour high-speed train from Chongqing to Zhangjiajie, a stunning mountain area in Hunan known for its dramatic quartz sandstone peaks. Settle into your hotel near the scenic area and try local Tujia cuisine in the evening.

Day 04
May 23
Zhangjiajie — Tianmen Mountain
▾
🗺Tianmen Mountain — Cable car ride → Natural arch cave → Glass Plank Road
✈ TransportPrivate car
Tianmen Mountain — Zhangjiajie’s iconic 'Gateway to Heaven' with a huge natural arch cave at 1,518 m above sea level
World’s longest alpine cable car — Sweeping valley views
Glass Plank Road — Thrilling cliffside walkway

Visit Tianmen Mountain, Zhangjiajie’s iconic 'Gateway to Heaven,' featuring a huge natural arch cave at 1,518 m above sea level. Ride the world’s longest alpine cable car for sweeping valley views, then walk the thrilling cliffside Glass Plank Road.

Day 05
May 24
Zhangjiajie National Forest Park — Tianzi Mountain • Yuanjiajie
▾
🗺Tianzi Mountain → Yuanjiajie
✈ TransportPark shuttle / private car
Tianzi Mountain — Famous for its sea of jagged sandstone peaks, with unbeatable sunrise and cloud-sea views
Yuanjiajie — Home to the Avatar Hallelujah Mountain, the real-life inspiration for the floating peaks in the movie Avatar

Explore two of Zhangjiajie National Forest Park’s most legendary sites: Tianzi Mountain, renowned for its sea of jagged sandstone peaks and spectacular sunrise/cloud-sea vistas; and Yuanjiajie, home to the Avatar Hallelujah Mountain — the real-life inspiration for the floating peaks in James Cameron’s Avatar.

Day 06
May 25
Zhangjiajie National Forest Park — Huangshizhai • Jinbian Stream
▾
🗺Huangshizhai → Jinbian Stream
✈ TransportPark shuttle / private car
Huangshizhai — The largest viewing platform in Zhangjiajie, offering panoramic views of the peak forest landscape
Jinbian Stream — A serene 7.5 km valley known as 'the most beautiful stream in the world,' with crystal water, lush forest, and quiet trails

Begin the day at Huangshizhai, the largest viewing platform in Zhangjiajie, offering panoramic views of the peak forest landscape. Then descend into the tranquil Jinbian Stream — a serene 7.5 km valley celebrated as 'the most beautiful stream in the world,' with crystal-clear water, lush forest, and peaceful walking trails.

Day 07
May 26
Zhangjiajie — Grand Canyon + Huanglong Cave
▾
🗺Zhangjiajie Grand Canyon → Huanglong Cave
✈ TransportPrivate car
Zhangjiajie Grand Canyon — Features the world’s longest and highest glass bridge (430 m long, 300 m high) and dramatic cliffside trails
Huanglong Cave — A vast 'underground karst palace' with illuminated stalactites, stalagmites, and an underground river explored by boat

Spend the day exploring two contrasting natural wonders: the vertiginous Zhangjiajie Grand Canyon, home to the world’s longest and highest glass bridge and dramatic cliffside trails; and the mystical Huanglong Cave, a vast 'underground karst palace' filled with illuminated stalactites, stalagmites, and an underground river navigated by boat.

Day 08
May 27
Departure
▾
🗺Hotel → Souvenir shopping → Zhangjiajie Airport
✈ TransportPrivate car
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Local souvenirs — Wild tea and Tujia embroidery
Zhangjiajie Airport — Flight departure

After breakfast and checkout, pick up some local souvenirs such as wild tea or Tujia embroidery. Then transfer to Zhangjiajie Airport for your flight back home, ending your journey through Chongqing’s 8D magic and Zhangjiajie’s stunning peaks.
